refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall automatically derive performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from worksite mission context and operational parameters, record them in a persistent, auditable baseline, and provide managed updates with traceability to source worksite inputs and calibration events.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was vague about 'method' and lacked specificity on persistence, auditability, and traceability—critical for safety-critical systems. Refinement adds explicit mention of safety margins (high safety criticality), baseline persistence, and traceability linkage to worksite inputs and calibration. This ensures the requirement is verifiable and testable.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall confirm: (1) performance targets are automatically computed from worksite mission inputs; (2) baseline records include timestamp, source inputs, and derivation method; (3) calibration updates are logged with delta and justification; (4) traceability chain from worksite input → derived target → baseline record is complete and auditable; (5) safety margins are explicitly included in derived targets.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: The system shall accept worksite mission context inputs (site geometry, soil conditions, task type, safety constraints) and operational parameters (operator skill level, equipment configuration).

CR-NGHE-005.2: The system shall compute performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) using a documented derivation algorithm based on mission context and operational parameters.
CR-NGHE-005.3: The system shall record and persist performance baselines with full traceability metadata (timestamp, source inputs, derivation method, safety margin justification).
CR-NGHE-005.4: The system shall manage baseline updates and calibration events, logging all changes with delta, reason, and authorization.
CR-NGHE-005.5: The system shall provide query and audit interfaces to retrieve baseline history and verify traceability from worksite input to recorded target.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized Performance Derivation Engine—a dedicated algorithmic module that ingests worksite mission context and operational parameters, applies a documented derivation algorithm (e.g., physics-based or empirical model), computes targets, and persists baselines with full metadata in a versioned database.
DP-B: Distributed Derivation—embed performance derivation logic in Vehicle and Teleoperation modules independently, with eventual synchronization via a shared baseline registry.
DP-C: Manual Baseline Entry with Validation—operators or site managers manually enter performance targets via a UI form; system validates against worksite constraints and records with metadata.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Centralized Performance Derivation Engine with versioned baseline persistence and full traceability metadata.

potential_sources: Coupling to worksite mission context input interface (Tasking) is necessary and justified—the derivation engine must ingest mission parameters.
Coupling to Safety Services is necessary for safety margin computation and validation—acceptable as it is a logical dependency.
Coupling to Vehicle and Teleoperation is indirect (via Telemetry for operational parameters) and loose—acceptable.
coupling_check.notes: DP-A exhibits low coupling to physical implementation details and high cohesion around the derivation and persistence concern. The algorithm is encapsulated, allowing future refinement without cascading changes. Distributed alternatives (DP-B) would introduce coupling duplication and synchronization complexity; manual entry (DP-C) would eliminate automation and auditability.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Worksite mission context is available in a structured, machine-readable format (e.g., via Tasking interface). Risk: If mission inputs are unstructured or incomplete, derivation accuracy will degrade. Mitigation: Define strict schema for mission context inputs.

Assumption: A documented, validated derivation algorithm exists or can be developed. Risk: Algorithm development and validation may require domain expertise and extended testing. Mitigation: Engage domain experts early; prototype algorithm with historical worksite data.

Assumption: Baseline persistence infrastructure (database, versioning) is available. Risk: If not, implementation will require additional development. Mitigation: Confirm infrastructure availability in design phase.

Assumption: Safety margin computation can be automated. Risk: Safety-critical margins may require human judgment or regulatory approval. Mitigation: Implement safety margin as a configurable parameter with audit trail; require safety manager sign-off on baseline.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-A to explicitly state: (1) the derivation algorithm shall be documented and version-controlled; (2) baseline persistence shall support full audit trail (immutable records, change logs); (3) safety margin computation shall be configurable and require safety manager validation; (4) the engine shall expose a Tasking input interface and a Telemetry input interface for operational parameters.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system boundary. It contains all subsystems and orchestrates the derivation, recording, and management of performance requirements. The Performance Derivation Engine (DP-A) is logically assigned to NGHE Solution as the system-level capability, with delegation to specialized sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) for input provision and validation.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Worksite mission context inputs (site geometry, soil conditions, task type, safety constraints, operator skill level, equipment configuration) flow into the Performance Derivation Engine via the Tasking interface.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Operational parameters (current equipment state, performance metrics, environmental conditions) flow from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to the Performance Derivation Engine via Telemetry interface for real-time baseline adjustment and validation.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety Services validates derived safety margins and alerts the Performance Derivation Engine if targets violate safety constraints. The engine may request safety margin re-computation via SafetyAlert.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Derived baselines and performance targets are transmitted to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules (and potentially to remote monitoring systems) via Network Link for operational use and tele-operator awareness.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level boundary and the natural home for the Performance Derivation Engine. The engine is a core system capability that orchestrates inputs from multiple subsystems and produces outputs consumed across the solution. Containment is very high; minimal diffusion risk.

Vehicle module is responsible for physical equipment operation and telemetry. While it provides operational parameters (Telemetry), it should not own the derivation algorithm or baseline persistence—these are system-level concerns. Assigning DP-A to Vehicle would couple the vehicle to system-level performance management logic and create diffusion if multiple vehicle types exist.

Teleoperation module is responsible for remote operation and tele-operator interface. It may consume derived performance targets (via Network Link) but should not own derivation logic. Assigning DP-A to Teleoperation would create tight coupling between remote operation and performance management, limiting reusability and creating diffusion across tele-operation scenarios.

Safety Services is responsible for safety validation and alerting. It plays a critical role in validating derived safety margins, but the overall derivation engine (DP-A) is broader than safety alone—it includes cycle time, energy efficiency, and availability. Assigning DP-A to Safety Services would create scope creep and diffusion of non-safety performance concerns into a safety-focused module.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Performance Derivation Engine (DP-A) is best contained within the NGHE Solution module as a system-level capability. This placement ensures: (1) the engine has visibility to all mission context inputs (Tasking) and operational parameters (Telemetry) across subsystems; (2) derived baselines are managed at the system level with full traceability and audit trail; (3) the engine can coordinate with Safety Services for safety margin validation without creating circular dependencies; (4) outputs (Network Link) can be distributed to Vehicle and Teleoperation without tight coupling; (5) future extensions (e.g., multi-vehicle coordination, fleet-level performance management) are supported without refactoring module boundaries.
containment_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution module is too coarse-grained, the Performance Derivation Engine may be difficult to test and maintain in isolation. Mitigation: Define the engine as a well-encapsulated sub-component within NGHE Solution with clear interfaces (Tasking in, Telemetry in, SafetyAlert inout, Network Link out).

Risk: If worksite mission context (Tasking) is not reliably available or is in an unstructured format, the derivation engine will fail or produce unreliable targets. Mitigation: Establish strict schema and validation for Tasking inputs; implement fallback logic (e.g., default targets) if inputs are incomplete.

Risk: If Safety Services validation (SafetyAlert) is slow or blocking, baseline derivation may be delayed, impacting operational responsiveness. Mitigation: Implement asynchronous validation; allow provisional baselines with safety manager sign-off required before operational use.

Risk: If baseline persistence infrastructure is not available or is unreliable, audit trail and traceability will be compromised. Mitigation: Confirm infrastructure availability and reliability in design phase; implement redundancy and backup for baseline records.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Refine IM assignment to specify: (1) the Performance Derivation Engine shall be a well-defined sub-component of NGHE Solution with a clear API (inputs: Tasking, Telemetry; outputs: Network Link; validation: SafetyAlert); (2) baseline persistence shall be implemented via a dedicated data store (e.g., versioned database) owned by NGHE Solution; (3) the engine shall expose query and audit interfaces for traceability verification; (4) Safety Services shall be invoked asynchronously for safety margin validation, with provisional baselines allowed pending approval.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FR (CR-NGHE-005 Refined) clearly specifies what the system must do: derive, record, and manage performance requirements with traceability and auditability. DP-A (Centralized Performance Derivation Engine) directly addresses this requirement by providing an algorithmic method to compute targets from mission context, persist them with metadata, and manage updates. The mapping is one-to-one and non-ambiguous. The DP is implementable and testable.

diffusion_risks: Low diffusion risk: DP-A is assigned to NGHE Solution, which is the appropriate system-level boundary. The engine is not duplicated across Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services modules.

Interface diffusion is minimal: the engine consumes Tasking and Telemetry inputs and produces Network Link outputs—all standard interfaces. SafetyAlert is used for validation, which is a logical dependency, not diffusion.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment score for NGHE Solution is 95/100, indicating very high suitability. The engine is a core system capability that naturally belongs at the solution level. No alternative module (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) has a containment score above 40, confirming that NGHE Solution is the clear choice.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of CR-NGHE-005 is feasible via the NGHE Solution module. Test cases can be designed to: (1) provide structured worksite mission inputs via Tasking; (2) verify that performance targets are computed correctly; (3) confirm that baselines are persisted with full metadata; (4) validate traceability from input to baseline record; (5) test calibration updates and change logging. All verification activities can be conducted within the NGHE Solution boundary with support from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services for input/output validation.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: CR-NGHE-005 is allocated to NGHE Solution. Sub-requirements (CR-NGHE-005.1 through CR-NGHE-005.5) are allocated as follows: CR-NGHE-005.1 (mission context input) is allocated to Tasking interface and Vehicle/Teleoperation for parameter provision; CR-NGHE-005.2 (target computation) is allocated to the Performance Derivation Engine within NGHE Solution; CR-NGHE-005.3 (baseline recording) is allocated to NGHE Solution data store; CR-NGHE-005.4 (baseline management) is allocated to NGHE Solution; CR-NGHE-005.5 (query/audit) is allocated to NGHE Solution query interface.

recommended_revisions: Clarify the derivation algorithm specification: define the mathematical or empirical model used to compute performance targets from mission context. This should be documented in a separate design specification and referenced in DP-A.

Specify the baseline persistence schema: define the data structure for baseline records, including fields for timestamp, source inputs, derivation method, safety margin justification, and change history. This should be part of the NGHE Solution data model.

Define the Tasking interface schema: specify the exact format and content of worksite mission context inputs (e.g., JSON schema, XML schema, or database schema). This is critical for the derivation engine to function correctly.

Establish safety manager approval workflow: define the process by which Safety Services validates derived safety margins and approves baselines for operational use. This should be documented in a separate operational procedure.

Plan for algorithm validation: allocate resources and schedule for validating the derivation algorithm against historical worksite data and expert judgment. This is a critical risk mitigation activity.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-005 Refined is a complex, multi-faceted requirement that spans multiple concerns: mission context input, algorithmic derivation, persistence, auditability, and traceability. The refined FR has been decomposed into five sub-requirements (CR-NGHE-005.1 through CR-NGHE-005.5), each of which is more atomic and addressable. DP-A (Centralized Performance Derivation Engine) is a sound solution concept, but its realization requires further decomposition to address: (1) the derivation algorithm specification; (2) the baseline persistence mechanism; (3) the Tasking interface schema; (4) the safety validation workflow; (5) the query/audit interface. These are distinct design concerns that should be addressed at the next decomposition level. The IM assignment to NGHE Solution is appropriate and passes all gates, but the implementation details (sub-modules, internal interfaces, data structures) require further specification. Therefore, the decision is to ZIGZAG_DOWN and decompose the sub-requirements.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: The system shall accept worksite mission context inputs (site geometry, soil conditions, task type, safety constraints) and operational parameters (operator skill level, equipment configuration).

CR-NGHE-005.2: The system shall compute performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) using a documented derivation algorithm based on mission context and operational parameters.
CR-NGHE-005.3: The system shall record and persist performance baselines with full traceability metadata (timestamp, source inputs, derivation method, safety margin justification).
CR-NGHE-005.4: The system shall manage baseline updates and calibration events, logging all changes with delta, reason, and authorization.
CR-NGHE-005.5: The system shall provide query and audit interfaces to retrieve baseline history and verify traceability from worksite input to recorded target.

assumptions: Worksite mission context is available in a structured, machine-readable format via the Tasking interface.
A documented, validated derivation algorithm exists or can be developed with domain expert input.
Baseline persistence infrastructure (database, versioning) is available or can be implemented.
Safety margin computation can be automated with configurable parameters and human validation.
Safety Services module is available and can provide validation via SafetyAlert interface.
Vehicle and Teleoperation modules can provide operational parameters via Telemetry interface.
Network Link interface is available for distributing derived baselines to subsystems.
open_risks: Algorithm Development Risk: The derivation algorithm may require significant domain expertise and validation effort. Mitigation: Engage domain experts early; prototype with historical data.
Data Quality Risk: If worksite mission context inputs are incomplete or unstructured, derivation accuracy will degrade. Mitigation: Define strict schema and validation for Tasking inputs.
Safety Validation Risk: Safety margin computation may require human judgment or regulatory approval, creating bottlenecks. Mitigation: Implement asynchronous validation with provisional baselines.
Infrastructure Risk: Baseline persistence infrastructure may not be available or reliable. Mitigation: Confirm availability and implement redundancy.
Interface Availability Risk: Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, and Network Link interfaces may not be fully specified or available. Mitigation: Coordinate with interface owners to ensure availability.

Traceability Risk: Maintaining full traceability from worksite input to baseline record may be complex and resource-intensive. Mitigation: Design traceability into the data model from the start; implement automated logging.
evidence_needed: Derivation Algorithm Specification: A detailed document describing the mathematical or empirical model used to compute performance targets from mission context. This should include validation results against historical worksite data.
Tasking Interface Schema: A formal specification (e.g., JSON schema, XML schema) defining the format and content of worksite mission context inputs.
Baseline Persistence Design: A data model specification defining the structure of baseline records, including fields for metadata, change history, and audit trail.
Safety Validation Workflow: A documented procedure describing how Safety Services validates derived safety margins and approves baselines for operational use.
Query/Audit Interface Specification: A formal specification of the query and audit interfaces, including API definitions and example queries.
Test Plan: A comprehensive test plan for CR-NGHE-005 and its sub-requirements, including test cases for derivation accuracy, persistence, traceability, and auditability.
Prototype or Proof-of-Concept: A working prototype or proof-of-concept demonstrating the feasibility of the derivation engine and baseline persistence mechanism.
